pi_items.source: Verified from verifier.py which checks if a repository with key 'maven-central-proxy' exists in the API response.
Verified from verifier.py which checks if target.get('type', '').upper() == 'REMOTE'.
Verified from task.json description which explicitly asks the agent to 'select Maven as the package type'. The verifier does not check this directly, but it is a core task requirement.
Verified from verifier.py which checks if 'repo1.maven.org/maven2' is in the repository URL. Confirmed via curl request that this is the valid and active Maven Central repository URL.
Verified from task.json description which explicitly asks the agent to 'add the description Proxy for Maven Central Repository'.
Verified from task_utils.sh and setup_artifactory.sh which set ADMIN_USER='admin', as well as verifier.py which uses '-u admin:password'.
Verified from task_utils.sh and setup_artifactory.sh which set ADMIN_PASS='password', as well as verifier.py which uses '-u admin:password'.
Verified from task_utils.sh and setup_artifactory.sh which set ARTIFACTORY_URL='http://localhost:8082', matching the default Artifactory OSS port 8082.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a new remote repository in JFrog Artifactory running at http://localhost:8082 using the default credentials (admin / password). The target repository must have the key 'maven-central-proxy', be of type 'remote', use the 'maven' package type, point to the valid Maven Central URL 'https://repo1.maven.org/maven2', and have the description 'Proxy for Maven Central Repository'. The verifier directly checks the repository key, type, and remote URL via the Artifactory REST API.
